subtitle_en: Shoot of David — Order of the Generations
description_en: A Hebrew world chronicle in two parts by Rabbi David Gans (student of the Rema and the Maharal of Prague). Part I traces Jewish history year by year from Creation to the author's day. Part II surveys general world history. First printed in Prague in 1592 — the first history book printed in Hebrew.

parts.description: Year-by-year chronicle of Jewish history from Creation through the sages of Gans's generation.
General / gentile history arranged by the four kingdoms of Daniel, with a famous preface defending the study of secular history.

source.note: Public-domain Hebrew text. English on this site is an independent machine translation with human review pending — not derived from other modern English editions.

stats.target_scope: Full Part I chronological almanac, then Part II
